sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 5.40 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.5)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a countable group acting on a set$$. Suppose that$G$is$k$-fold transitive for every finite$k$, and$G$contains no non-trivial permutations of finite support. Is it true that$$can be identified with the rational line$$in such a way that$G$becomes a group of autohomeomorphisms? { P.\,M.\,Neumann} Not always (A.\,H.\,Mekler, J. London Math. Soc., { 33} (1986), 49--58).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
